---
title: "Quarto 1 — Your First .qmd"
subtitle: "What you already know + Quarto-specific additions"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Where we are

::: {.incremental}
- Morning: R Markdown essentials ✓
- Just now: the bridge ✓
- Now: Quarto fundamentals (this deck)
- After lunch: tables, figures, equations, slides, projects
:::

## You already know

YAML + markdown + chunks. We're swapping:

::: {.incremental}
- `output:` → `format:`
- Chunk options to `#|` lines
- File extension `.qmd`
:::

# Quarto authoring workflow {.section-divider}

## On Posit Cloud

::: {.incremental}
- **File → New File → Quarto Document**
- **Render** button (where Knit used to be)
- `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+K` works for both
- Same four-pane RStudio interface
:::

# YAML expanded {.section-divider}

## A realistic header

```yaml
---
title: "Serum PFOA and Cholesterol"
subtitle: "Triangle Region Biomonitoring, 2024"
author:
  - name: Jane Smith
    affiliation: NIEHS Biostatistics
  - name: John Doe
    affiliation: NIEHS Epidemiology
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
  docx: default
---
```

## Notice

::: {.incremental}
- Multi-author with `name` and `affiliation`
- `date: today` — no inline R needed
- Multiple formats stack under `format:`
- All hyphens, no underscores
:::

# Callouts — Quarto-only {.section-divider}

## Five types

```markdown
::: {.callout-note}
General info.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
A useful trick.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
Watch out.
:::
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Live example
Like this one.
:::

## R Markdown vs Quarto callouts

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**R Markdown**

```html
<div style="padding: 12px;
background-color: #F0FDF4;
border-left: 4px solid #0D9488;
margin: 16px 0;">
**Tip:** ...
</div>
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Quarto**

```markdown
::: {.callout-tip}
**Tip:** ...
:::
```
:::
::::

# Panel tabsets {.section-divider}

## Side-by-side R and Python

````markdown
::: {.panel-tabset}

### R
```{r}`r ''`
ggplot(data, aes(x, y)) + geom_point()
```

### Python
```{python}`r ''`
sns.scatterplot(data=data, x="x", y="y")
```

:::
````

::: {.fragment}
Tabs in HTML; sequential sections in Word/PDF.
:::

# Today's dataset {.section-divider}

## `biomarker_data`

::: {.incremental}
- Simulated NIEHS biomonitoring data
- N = 200 participants
- Variables: age, sex, site, PFOA, PFOS, BMI, cholesterol
- Same data your morning Module 04 was using
- Used throughout the afternoon
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Notebook Quarto 1

::: {.callout-tip}
Open `afternoon_quarto/01_first_document.qmd`. Work through Exercises 1.1 and 1.2.
:::

Back in 30 min — then we dive into chunks and tables.
